steps.title: Track the comparison
steps.content: The text compares ordinary historical knowledge to a map: it can be detailed, accurate, and useful, but it feels cold and lacks the vivid charm of a painting. The point is that historical figures are often known through facts rather than felt as full people.

steps.title: Eliminate narrower claims
steps.content:
• Choice A focuses on historians' interests, which the text does not discuss.
  • Choice B claims historians' creativity is restricted, but the text is about how historical knowledge feels.
  • Choice D treats the map-and-painting comparison too literally.
The remaining choice states the main idea about facts rather than personalities. The answer is C.
